# Transcrição: What_do_you_think_of_my_Texas_accent_

- **Fonte:** What_do_you_think_of_my_Texas_accent_
- **Duração:** 01:45
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:45]

When I was a young little boy, I said to my mom and dad, I said, mama, when I grow up, I'm going to be a cowboy. And I remember my mama looked me in the eyes and she goes, that's impossible, son. You weren't born in Texas. Not only were you not born in Texas, son, you don't know how to ride a damn horse. If you don't know how to ride a damn horse, how are you going to be a cowboy? One of the most powerful lessons I learned in the world of communication is that the way you speak is just a series of behaviors. That's all that it is. It's just a series of behaviors. And these series of behaviors that dictate how you currently communicate, you learn them unconsciously based on the people that you were inspired by when you were really young. You picked them up from mom, dad, your teachers, your uncles, your cousins, your aunties. You picked them up unconsciously. They're either serving you or they're not. And that's why I created a three-part video series to help you become conscious of those unconscious behaviors that may not be serving you in adulthood. So check out the three-part video series and learn how to improve your communication skills today. You know, mama, I got something to tell you. You always said it was impossible for me to be a cowboy because I couldn't ride a horse. You said it was impossible. Now, mama, let me tell you one thing. I don't ride horses. I'm going to ride a kangaroo. And you might be thinking, mama, you're thinking, how are you going to ride a kangaroo? I'm going to ride a kangaroo in the pouch. Oh yeah, I'm going to ride in the pouch. Change your communication skills today. It's just a series of behaviors. You can change them. Why not change them now?
